summary: End-to-end testing of Slabby platform (rebuilt from CardWise). Backend: 22/22 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All critical flows passed - registration redirected to marketplace, login worked, wallet deposit/withdraw reflected balance, card creation/publish worked, marketplace and razz browse pages showed correct data, provably fair server_seed_hash exposed.
passed_tests: API /api root, /api/health, /api/stats - PASS
POST /api/auth/register with new email returns 200 with access_token - PASS
POST /api/auth/login with seeded trader@slabby.com / password123 - PASS
POST /api/auth/login with invalid creds returns 401 - PASS
GET /api/auth/me returns user profile - PASS
GET /api/wallet returns wallet with $0 initial balance - PASS
POST /api/wallet/deposit increases available_balance - PASS
POST /api/wallet/withdraw decreases available_balance - PASS
POST /api/wallet/withdraw with overdraft returns 400 - PASS
GET /api/wallet/transactions returns deposit + withdrawal txns - PASS
POST /api/cards creates draft card - PASS
POST /api/cards/{id}/publish moves card to 'available' status with listed_at timestamp - PASS
GET /api/cards lists published cards in marketplace - PASS
GET /api/cards/my-cards returns user's cards - PASS
GET /api/cards?category=basketball filter works - PASS
POST /api/razz creates draft razz - PASS
POST /api/razz/{id}/publish activates razz with 64-char sha256 server_seed_hash - PASS
GET /api/razz lists active razzes - PASS
GET /api/razz/stats/active-count - PASS
GET /api/razz/{id} hides 'proof' field before completion - PASS
UI: Registration redirects to /marketplace - PASS
UI: Wallet deposit reflected in available-balance display ($0 -> $250) - PASS
UI: Wallet withdraw reflected ($250 -> $200) - PASS
UI: Card creation via Marketplace modal works (toast 'Card created!') - PASS
UI: Browse marketplace shows published cards - PASS
UI: Razz page shows active razzes with spot price and spots count - PASS
UI: 'Provably Fair' info banner visible on Razz page - PASS
critical_code_review_comments: Stripe deposit/withdrawal is MOCKED - wallet_service simulates instant deposit without real payment processor. Acceptable for MVP but must be flagged for production.
EmailStr (pydantic email-validator) rejects '.test' TLDs. If main agent intends to seed users programmatically, use real-looking domains like @slabby.com (which works) or @example.com.
razz_service generates server_seed_hash at create or publish time, but never exposes server_seed before draw (good). 'proof' correctly hidden until COMPLETED status.

Card 'My Cards' tab testid is generated dynamically as `tab-${tab.id}` - main agent should document or expose the actual tab IDs (likely 'browse-cards' / 'my-cards') for stable e2e selectors. Suggestion: add data-testid='my-cards-tab' explicitly.

Backend services use module-level singleton pattern via `from server import xxx_service` inside route dependency providers - this creates a circular import risk if the module loading order changes. Works for now but fragile.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on critical flows tested (registration, login redirect, wallet deposit/withdraw, card create+publish, marketplace browse, razz browse)

test_credentials.note: Seeded user is auto-created by test suite if not present

seed_data_creation: Test suite creates ephemeral users (trader_<uuid>@slabby.io) per run. Also ensures trader@slabby.com exists for seeded login test. Test cards prefixed with TEST_<uuid8>.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Slabby platform is fully functional. Backend uses event-sourced architecture with modular routes/services. JWT auth via Bearer token. Token stored as 'cardwise_token' (legacy key name) in localStorage. Auth response uses 'access_token' field. All wallet, card, razz CRUD flows verified. Razz provably-fair flow: server_seed_hash is published at razz creation/publish (sha256, 64 hex chars), server_seed and proof stay hidden until draw completes. Stripe is MOCKED - deposits/withdrawals are instant.

mocked_apis_list: Stripe Connect deposit/withdrawal - MOCKED, wallet_service updates balance instantly without real payment processing
